scenario: A Pinnacle payments architect documents the Camunda 8.8 COLLECT hit policy used in a SUM-style table running 45000 transactions per day. The team wants to know where exactly in the DMN XML the aggregator value SUM is placed when configuring a decisionTable element.
question: Where is the COLLECT aggregator value set in the DMN XML according to the Camunda 8 docs?

options.text: On the aggregation attribute of the decisionTable XML element, paired with the hitPolicy=COLLECT attribute on the same element, as shown in the docs' XML example
On an aggregation child element nested inside the decisionTable XML element, with the policy name written as the element's text content for the engine
On a separate aggregation attribute of each output XML element inside the decisionTable, with the aggregator value repeated per output column at deployment
On the aggregation attribute of the parent decision XML element rather than the decisionTable element, with the aggregator value applied to all decision tables

a.text: Correct. The COLLECT section states the aggregator is set as the aggregation attribute of the decisionTable XML element, and the example shows hitPolicy=COLLECT with aggregation=SUM on the same decisionTable element.

b.text: Incorrect. The docs do not introduce an aggregation child element; they place the aggregator on the aggregation attribute of the decisionTable XML element itself rather than inside a child element.

c.text: Incorrect. With an aggregated COLLECT policy, only one output is allowed, and the aggregator is placed once on the decisionTable element rather than repeated per output column XML element.

d.text: Incorrect. The docs place the aggregation attribute on the decisionTable XML element, not on the parent decision element; the parent decision element only carries the decision id and name.

explanation: The COLLECT section says the aggregator is set as the aggregation attribute of the decisionTable XML element. The accompanying XML example shows hitPolicy=COLLECT alongside aggregation=SUM on the decisionTable element.
